headline: Struggling Nike will cut around 1,400 jobs in efficiency push
summary: STORY: Nike said on Thursday it is laying off about 1,400 people to streamline workflows.As the struggling sportswear company fights through a years-long sales slump.In a memo to employees on Thursday, the company said it would cut jobs in global operations, mainly technology.It accounts for a little less than 2% of the global workforce.Shares edged up about 0.5% in after-hours trading but shares have lost more than half their value in the last three years.:: OnNimbler competitors like On, Hoka and Anta have won more shelf space.It is the latest in a string of job cuts.Most recently 775 roles were axed in January in a bid to speed up automation.:: NikeCEO Elliott Hill, who took the helm in 2024 has vowed to re-center the brand.Focusing on core sports like running and soccer, and bringing new and innovative shoes to market quickly.However, efforts to wow the market with new, must-have sneakers have been inconsistent.Analysts believe Nike should be further along in its recovery and the job losses are unsurprising.According to the memo, the cuts will allow Nike to better integrate its supply chains and to focus its technology operations in two main hubs in Oregon and India.

headline: Nike Cutting 1,400 More Jobs. The Stock Is Rising.
summary: Nike  told its employees worldwide that it is cutting more jobs as part of the final stretch of its Win Now action plan.  The footwear and athletic apparel company has been taking “deliberate steps to strengthen our foundation, sharpen how we compete, and build a model designed to deliver long-term profitable growth,” including aligning its teams, capabilities, and footprint for its future needs.  Nike’s shares were up 0.7% at $45.08 in late Thursday trading, after closing down 2% in regular trading.
